周四，阿里巴巴公布了自上市以来最慢的季度收入增长，未达到预期，但这家中国电商巨头的收入确实超过预期。
每股收益：每股16.87元（约合2.65美元），预计为每股16.18元，同比下降23%。
10%的收入增长率是该公司自2014年在美国上市以来最慢的季度同比增长率。
阿里巴巴股价在上午下跌，但随后减少了损失。该公司于周四结束&#39；中国股市交易日下跌0.7%。
阿里巴巴在中国一直面临着宏观经济方面的阻力，这给这家电子商务巨头&#39；这是生意。例如，今年第四季度，中国的零售销售仍然疲软。而且中国的竞争加剧了#39；中国的电子商务空间。
与此同时，该公司&#39；随着中国收紧对中国的监管，中国股市在去年下跌了50%以上；在从反垄断到数据保护等领域，中国的技术部门。去年，作为反垄断调查的一部分，阿里巴巴被监管机构处以182.3亿元（28亿美元）的罚款。
"我们目前的股价并不能公正地反映公司的价值。按照目前的价格水平，我们计划继续回购股票。同时，我们将保持强劲的现金头寸，为未来的投资提供财务灵活性；阿里巴巴首席财务官Toby Xu在财报电话会议上说。
在去年12月的一个季度，阿里巴巴以约14亿美元的价格回购了约1010万股在美国上市的美国存托股票。其股份回购计划将于2022年12月底到期。
投资者正在关注一些关键指标，包括客户管理收入、云计算收入和公司&#39；这是向前的指导。
在去年12月的一个季度，阿里巴巴进一步细分了不同业务领域的业绩报告方式。而不是&#34；核心商务&#34；该公司现在将其中国和国际零售业务划分为不同的报告类别。该公司还公布了其物流部门菜鸟和当地消费者服务的数据，其中包括其食品配送平台ele。我阿里巴巴已经开始报告每个细分市场的息税前、折旧和摊销前调整后收益。
在其中国商务业务中，客户管理收入是分析师的一个关键指标。它是阿里巴巴最大的一部分#39；这是我们的收入。CMR为1009亿元，同比下降1%。
CMR是阿里巴巴从其淘宝和天猫电商平台上销售给商家的营销等服务中获得的收入。
在去年12月的第三季度，中国举办了一年一度的光棍节购物节，这是一场为期多日的盛会，零售商提供了巨大的折扣。阿里巴巴表示，在11天的时间里，其商品总额达到创纪录的5403亿元。
但GMV实际上是一个平台上交易的金额，并不能转化为阿里巴巴的直接收入。
同时，公司收入#39；去年12月，中国国际商务业务同比增长18%，达到164.5亿元。
阿里巴巴表示，来自欠发达地区的客户比例持续增长。这家总部位于杭州的公司一直专注于渗透中国较小的城市，寻找新的增长途径。该公司推出了一款名为淘宝交易（Taobao Deals）的产品，提供打折产品，以帮助实现其目标。阿里巴巴表示，去年12月季度，淘宝交易的支付订单同比增长100%。
12月第三季度的云计算收入为195.4亿元，同比增长20%。这比9月份季度33%的增长率要慢。但云部门的亏损有所收窄。
有两个因素一直在影响云业务的业绩。TikTok所有者ByteDance继续将其海外业务从阿里巴巴转移出去&#39；中国&#39；阿里巴巴对游戏和教育等行业的监管打击也将影响业绩。游戏和教育是阿里巴巴的主要客户。
但该公司继续将其客户群多样化，转向其他行业。来自非互联网行业的收入占云收入的52%。
面对日益激烈的竞争，阿里巴巴一直在寻求发展一些较新的业务，如食品配送。食品配送隶属于阿里巴巴当地的消费者服务部门，包括旅游应用Fliggy等其他产品。
去年12月，本地消费服务收入为121.4亿元，同比增长27%。
食物递送服务ele。我，阿里巴巴说&#34；每订单的单位经济性同比改善#34；由&#34驱动；严格控制用户购买支出，降低交付成本"
note_en:
Alibaba on Thursday reported its slowest quarterly revenue growth since going public and missed expectations, but the Chinese e-commerce giant did beat on earnings.
  EPS: 16.87 ($2.65) yuan per share vs.  16.18 yuan per share estimated , a 23 % year-on-year fall.
  The 10% revenue growth is the slowest quarterly year-on-year growth rate for the company since its 2014 U.S. listing.
 Alibaba shares dropped in the morning, but then curtailed their losses. The company ended Thursday's trading session down 0.7%.
 Alibaba has been facing macroeconomic headwinds in China, which have weighed on the e-commerce giant's business. Chinese  retail sales remained sluggish in the fourth quarter of the year, for example. And there is heightened competition in China's e-commerce space.
 Meanwhile, the company's shares have fallen over 50% in the last year as China tightened regulation on the country's technology sector in areas from  antitrust to  data protection. Last year, Alibaba was hit with a 18.23 billion yuan ($2.8 billion)  fine by regulators as  part of an antitrust investigation.
   "Our current share price does not fairly reflect the value of the company. At current price levels, we plan on continuing our share repurchases. At the same time, we will maintain a strong cash position that gives us the financial flexibility for future investments," Toby Xu, chief financial officer of Alibaba, said on the earnings conference call.
 In the December quarter, Alibaba bought back approximately 10.1 million of its U.S.-listed American depositary shares for around $1.4 billion. Its share repurchase scheme will expire at the end of December 2022.
  Investors are watching a few key metrics including customer management revenue, cloud computing revenue and the company's forward guidance.
 In the December quarter, Alibaba further broke down the way it reports results for different segments of its business. Instead of "core commerce," the company now splits up its China and international retail businesses into different reporting categories. It has also broken out figures for its logistics arm, Cainiao, and local consumer service, which includes its food delivery platform ele.me. And Alibaba has started to report adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization for each segment.
 Under its China commerce business, customer management revenue is a key metric for analysts. It is the single biggest portion of Alibaba's revenue. CMR came in at 100.09 billion yuan, a 1% year-on-year fall.
 CMR is revenue Alibaba gets from services such as marketing that the company sells to merchants on its Taobao and Tmall e-commerce platforms.
 During the December quarter, China had its annual Singles Day shopping festival, a multiday extravaganza in which retailers offer huge discounts. Alibaba said gross merchandise volume during the 11-day period  hit a record 540.3 billion yuan.
 But GMV, effectively the amount of money transacted across a platform, does not translate into direct revenue for Alibaba.
  Meanwhile, revenue for the company's international commerce business grew 18% year-over-year to 16.45 billion yuan in the December quarter.
 Alibaba said the percentage of customers from less-developed areas has continued to grow. The Hangzhou-headquartered company has been focused on penetrating smaller Chinese cities to find new avenues of growth. It has launched a product called Taobao Deals, which offers discounted products, to help with its aim. Alibaba said paid orders on Taobao Deals grew 100% year-on-year in the December quarter.
  Cloud computing revenue for the December quarter came in at 19.54 billion yuan, up 20% year-on-year. That was slower than the 33% growth seen in the September quarter. But losses for the cloud division narrowed.
 Two factors have been weighing on the cloud business results. TikTok-owner ByteDance has continued to move its overseas operations off of Alibaba's cloud products while China's regulatory crackdown on sectors including gaming and education — key customers for Alibaba — will also weigh on results.
 But the company has continued to diversify its customer base to other industries. Revenue from noninternet industries accounted for 52% of the cloud revenue.
  In the face of rising competition, Alibaba has looked to grow some of its newer businesses such as food delivery, which is under its local consumer services division and includes other products such as travel app Fliggy.
 Revenue for local consumer services came in at 12.14 billion yuan in the December quarter, up 27% year-on-year.
 For food delivery service ele.me, Alibaba said "unit economics per order improved year-over-year" driven by "disciplined user acquisitions spending and reduced delivery cost."
title: 阿里巴巴报告称，第三季度营收同比增长10%，至380.6亿美元，为2014年美国IPO以来的最低季度增长，净收入同比下降74%，至约32亿美元
title_en: Alibaba reports Q3 revenue rose 10% YoY to $38.06B, the slowest quarterly growth since its 2014 US IPO, and net income fell 74% YoY to ~$3.2B

note:
总部位于伦敦的网络安全公司Red Sift在B轮融资中筹集了5400万美元。该公司打算利用这笔资金进一步推动全球扩张，其中一个突出表现就是计划在德克萨斯州奥斯汀开设一家美国总部办公室。自2016年以来，Red Sift已筹集到6980万美元。
网络钓鱼和勒索软件攻击曾经是由大流行推动的活动激增，如今已成为常态。为了应对这一挑战，并帮助企业将这些威胁控制在最低限度，Red Sift提供了三种解决方案，专门针对最常被锁定的目标：营销、信息安全、法律和合规团队。
该公司的OnDMARC服务提供防止域名冒充的保护，而OnINBOX通过在每封电子邮件中提供安全分析来加强网络钓鱼培训。现在，更进一步，Red Sift最近发布了OnDOMAIN，这是一个品牌防御服务，允许IT安全团队快速关闭钓鱼网站，根除合法但不安全的域名。
Red Sift的报价如此引人注目，以至于该公司目前在其客户群中拥有700多个全球品牌，包括Wise、Telefonica和pipedrive，仅去年一年的年度经常性收入就翻了一番以上。
Red Sift价值5400万美元的B轮融资由Highland Europe牵头，包括Sands Capital、Oxford Capital和MMC Ventures在内的新投资者和现有投资者参与。
金沙资本的迈克尔·格拉宁格评论道，“红SIFT仍然是它的客户真正的合作伙伴，通过提供一个平台来保护组织免受多个、多个和相互关联的威胁向量的侵害，我们很乐意继续支持Red SIFT，因为它在多个产品上进行创新，所有产品都有一个坚实的、企业准备好的基础。”
note_en:
London-based cybersecurity company  Red Sift has raised $54 million in a Series B round. The company intends to use the funding to further drive global expansion efforts, highlighted by a planned opening of a US headquarter office in Austin, Texas. Since 2016, Red Sift has raised $69.8 million.
 What once was a pandemic-driven spike in activity, phishing and ransomware attacks have become a regularity. Rising to this challenge and helping organisations keep these threats to the bottom line at bay, Red Sift offers a trio of solutions specifically designed for those most often targeted: marketing, information security, and legal and compliance teams.
 The company’s  OnDMARC service offers protection against domain impersonations, while  OnINBOX reinforces phishing training by providing a security analysis inside every email. Now, going one step further, Red Sift has recently released,  OnDOMAIN, a brand defense service that allows IT security teams to quickly shut down phishing sites and root out legitimate, but unsecured domains.
 So compelling is Red Sift’s offer that the company now counts over 700 global brands amongst its customer base, including Wise, Telefonica, and pipedrive, and in the last year alone has seen annual recurring revenue numbers more than double.
 Red Sift’s $54 million Series B round was led by  Highland Europe and saw participation from new and existing investors including  Sands Capital,  Oxford Capital, and  MMC Ventures.
 Sands Capital’s  Michael Graninger comments, “Red Sift continues to be a genuine partner to its customers in countering the bad guys by offering a platform to protect organisations from multiple, varied and interconnected threat vectors. We’re happy to continue to support Red Sift as it innovates across multiple products that all share a solid, enterprise-ready foundation.”
title: 总部位于伦敦的Red Sift为营销、信息安全和法律团队提供网络安全工具，它筹集了5400万美元的B系列资金，并计划在美国扩张
title_en: London-based Red Sift, which offers cybersecurity tools for marketing, information security, and legal teams, raises a $54M Series B and plans a US expansion

note:
总部位于西雅图的电子商务技术初创公司Fabric宣布了一轮1.4亿美元的C系列融资，由软银牵头，估值约15亿美元。
这笔交易使Fabric成为西雅图地区最新的“独角兽”，这是一家价值超过10亿美元的私营初创公司。这是该地区第18家实现这一目标的公司。到目前为止，Fabric总共筹集了2.935亿美元。
Faisal Masud首席执行官费萨尔·马苏德在接受采访时表示，他相信，随着数万亿美元的电子商务进入网络，这家拥有300人的公司才刚刚开始挖掘其潜在的市场机会。
亚马逊、Alphabet、Groupon、Staples和eBay的资深人士马苏德（Masud）说：“有这么多荒谬的增长正在等待实现。”。“坦率地说，我们认为自己非常渺小，我们将在很长一段时间内保持这种想法。”
这家300人的公司为零售商和企业对企业品牌提供软件、API和其他幕后技术，用于在线商务的许多方面，包括定价/促销、库存管理、数字店面和支付/结账。
这样做的目的是让中端市场的品牌即使在缺乏内部技术专业知识或能力来构建强大的电子商务系统的情况下，也能与亚马逊保持同步。
主要客户包括奇科服装、麦当劳、TriMark餐厅供应、L'Azurd珠宝，以及最新宣布的由女演员杰西卡·阿尔巴（Jessica Alba）共同创立的可持续消费品企业诚实公司。
Fabric的技术与Salesforce、Shopify和Adobe Commerce（Magento）等公司的产品竞争。在许多情况下，它提供了定制内部技术的替代方案。
在Fabric完成价值4300万美元的B轮融资不到一年后，该公司的估值为8.5亿美元。马苏德表示，该公司在前一轮融资中没有花太多钱，也没有积极寻求额外投资，但决定在估值符合预期时继续进行。
通过其愿景基金2进行投资的软银，与先行者风险投资公司、Glynn Capital和现有投资者Redpoint Ventures、Norwest Ventures和Stripes一起参与了这轮投资。软银投资总监罗伯特·卡普兰（Robert Kaplan）将作为投资的结果加入Fabric董事会。
该公司将利用这些资金继续其地域扩张，特别关注欧洲、中东和非洲，调整其技术以支持新的语言和货币，并为这些地区的团队配备人员。此外，该公司还希望在其商业工具中增加新的智能和自主性。
Fabric自认为是“商业AWS”，马苏德说，他指的是亚马逊网络服务云部门，该部门为世界各地的公司提供基础技术基础设施。
该公司的做法在业内被普遍称为“无头”商务，将后端技术与前端体验分离，让品牌在构建系统和客户体验方面具有灵活性。
Fabric由瑞安·巴特利（Ryan Bartley）和希夫·阿加瓦尔（Shiv Agarwal）于2017年创立。马苏德（Masud）担任首席数字官时，他们曾是斯台普斯（Staples）的技术领导者，后来又担任这家办公用品巨头的首席技术官。马苏德在2000年接替阿加瓦尔担任首席执行官。巴特利仍然是该公司的联合创始人和传教士。
Fabric的c-suite包括来自亚马逊和其他公司的商业、技术和零售老手：
最近被任命为Fabric第一任首席运营官的斯泰西·萨尔（Stacy Saal）此前曾负责亚马逊Prime Air无人机交付业务，并在这家电子商务巨头工作了12年。
首席技术官乌默尔·萨迪克（Umer Sadiq）在亚马逊工作了近12年，担任过各种工程领导职务。
首席营销官凯伦·布鲁尔（Karen Brewer）曾长期担任Autodesk和其他科技公司的高管。
首席财务官内文·谢蒂（Nevin Shetty）是西雅图创业蓝图注册中心（Blueprint Registry）的首席执行官，该公司于2018年被大卫的新娘（David’s Bridal）收购。
首席人事官瓦尔·鲁普（Val Rupp）是惠普（HPE）和微软（Microsoft）的资深员工，在电子阅读器业务早期曾担任亚马逊Kindle部门的高级人力资源主管。
首席法律官Krupah Shah是Heidrick&amp；在职业生涯早期，她曾在Groupon担任公司法律顾问。
2000年代，马苏德在亚马逊工作了六年多，除了担任其他领导角色外，还帮助推出了亚马逊基础产品线。
截至1月中旬，GeekWire统计了西雅图地区16家独角兽初创公司。云开发工具公司Temporal上周以1.03亿美元的价格加入了该集团，Fabric的宣布又增加了一项。

note_en:
Fabric, a Seattle-based e-commerce technology startup whose leadership team includes several former Amazon executives,  announced a $140 million Series C funding round, led by SoftBank, at a valuation of about $1.5 billion.
 The deal makes Fabric the Seattle area’s newest “unicorn,” a privately held startup valued at more than a billion dollars. It’s the 18th company in the region to achieve that designation. Fabric has raised a total of $293.5 million to date.
 Fabric CEO  Faisal Masud said in an interview that he believes the 300-person company is just starting to tap its potential market opportunity as trillions of dollars in commerce moves online.
  “There’s this absurd amount of growth that is just waiting to be taken,” said Masud, a veteran of Amazon, Alphabet, Groupon, Staples and eBay. “Frankly, we think we’re very small, and we’re going to keep thinking that for a very long time.”
  The 300-person company offers software, APIs, and other behind-the-scenes technology used by retailers and business-to-business brands for many aspects of online commerce, including pricing/promotion, inventory management, digital storefronts, and payments/checkout.
 The idea is to give mid-market brands the tools keep up with Amazon even in cases when they don’t have the in-house technical expertise or capability to build out robust e-commerce systems.
 Major customers include Chico’s clothing, McDonald’s, TriMark restaurant supply, L’azurde jewelry and, newly announced, The Honest Company, the sustainable consumer goods venture co-founded by actress Jessica Alba.
  Fabric’s technology competes against offerings from companies such as Salesforce, Shopify and Adobe Commerce (Magento). In many cases, it provides an alternative to custom, in-house technology.
  The funding comes less than a year after Fabric closed  a $43 million Series B round that valued the company at $850 million. Masud said the company hasn’t spent much of that prior funding round, and hadn’t actively been seeking additional investment, but decided to go ahead when the valuation matched its expectations.
 SoftBank, which is making the investment through its Vision Fund 2, was joined in the round by Forerunner Ventures, Glynn Capital and existing investors Redpoint Ventures, Norwest Ventures and Stripes.  Robert Kaplan, SoftBank investment director, will join the Fabric board as a result of the investment.
   The company will use the funds to continue its geographic expansion, with a particular focus on Europe, the Middle East and Africa, adapting its technology to support new languages and currencies, and staffing up its teams in those regions. In addition, the company is looking to add new intelligence and autonomy into its commerce tools.
 Fabric thinks of itself as the “AWS for commerce,” Masud said, referring to the Amazon Web Services cloud division that provides the underlying technology infrastructure for companies around the world.
 The company’s approach is commonly known in the industry as “headless” commerce, separating back-end technology from front-end experiences to give brands flexibility in building out systems and customer experiences.
 Fabric was founded in 2017 by  Ryan Bartley and  Shiv Agarwal, who were previously technology leaders at Staples when Masud was chief digital officer and then chief technology officer at the office-supply giant. Masud succeeded Agarwal as CEO in 2000. Bartley remains with the company as co-founder and evangelist.
 Fabric’s c-suite includes business, technology and retail veterans from Amazon and other companies:
 Stacy Saal, recently named Fabric’s first chief operating officer, previously oversaw the Amazon Prime Air drone delivery business, among other roles in a 12-year career at the e-commerce giant.
 Umer Sadiq, chief technology officer, worked at Amazon for nearly 12 years in a variety of engineering leadership roles.
 Karen Brewer, chief marketing officer, was a longtime executive at Autodesk and other technology companies.
 Nevin Shetty, chief financial officer, was CEO of Seattle startup  Blueprint Registry, acquired by David’s Bridal in 2018.
 Val Rupp, chief people officer, is a veteran of HPE and Microsoft who was senior HR leader for Amazon’s Kindle division in the early days of the e-reader business.
 Krupah Shah, chief legal officer, was chief privacy officer at Heidrick & Struggles and worked as corporate counsel at Groupon earlier in her career.
 Masud spent more than six years at Amazon in the 2000s, helping to launch the Amazon Basics product line in addition to other leadership roles.
 GeekWire  counted 16 unicorn startups in the Seattle region as of mid-January. Temporal, a cloud developer tools company, joined the group  with its $103 million round last week, and Fabric’s announcement adds one more.

title: 总部位于西雅图的Fabric为零售商和B2B品牌提供电子商务工具，以约15亿美元的估值筹集了由软银的Vision Fund 2牵头的1.4亿美元的C系列

去年年底在中国发布的摩托罗拉Edge X30将作为摩托罗拉Edge Plus（或欧洲和其他市场的30 Pro）进入美国市场。它具有更高的终端规格比2021的摩托罗拉边缘提供，从Android旗舰芯片组杜JOR，SnAPang龙8 Gen 1。虽然摩托罗拉似乎已经解决了我们对这款中档手机的一些投诉，但这款手机的价格也要贵得多，为其功能设置了更高的门槛。
Edge Plus为2022年的旗舰手机检查了很多盒子：有一个6.7英寸1080p OLED，刷新率高达144Hz，低于6GHz的5G，包括C波段（Verizon版本的手机将有mmWave，解锁的型号不会），4800mAh电池，8GB或12GB RAM，快速30W有线充电和15W无线充电。摩托罗拉甚至在盒子里装了一个30W的充电器，这是多年前遗留下来的一个古怪的东西。
摩托罗拉还推出了一款新的智能触笔，与一个专门用来装触笔的盒子捆绑销售。它是三星笔中的一种主动触笔，比摩托罗拉Moto G触笔等手机上销售的简单被动触笔功能更强。和S笔一样，它对压力很敏感，可以作为遥控器进行某些操作，而无需将笔触碰屏幕。摩托罗拉表示，将在“未来几周”公布触笔和手机壳的价格和供货情况
Edge Plus的后面板上有两个5000万像素的摄像头：一个f/1.8标准宽的光学图像稳定摄像头和一个f/2.2超宽摄像头。主相机应该提供改进的自动对焦，这是2021边缘的弱点。手机后部三摄像头阵列上的第三个镜头是一个200万像素的深度传感器，其实用性值得怀疑。正面有一台6000万像素的自拍相机。
然而，规格表上有几个地方看起来并不乐观，尤其是1000美元的手机。耐候性等级仅为IP52，这意味着有一些防喷水保护，但不能像这一级别的IP68设备那样完全浸入水中。
手机的正面和背面都有玻璃，这很好，但分别是较旧的Gorilla glass 3和5。Pixel 6和Samsung S22 Plus都在正面使用了较新的Gorilla Glass Victus（S22的Victus Plus）来防止划痕和跌落。Edge Plus的外部框架采用聚碳酸酯塑料，而非铝。摩托罗拉表示，它将通过三年的安全更新来支持Edge Plus，这也低于安卓手机中最好的支持政策。
如果没有其他选择的话，Edge Plus是美国的另一个高端Android选项，在美国，我们可以选择的设备很少。Edge Plus可在“有限时间”内解锁100美元据摩托罗拉称，未来几个月，Verizon和其他运营商将提供该服务。
note_en:
The Motorola Edge X30, announced in China at the end of last year, is coming to the US as the Motorola Edge Plus (or the 30 Pro in Europe and other markets). It comes with higher-end specs than 2021’s Motorola Edge offered, starting with the Android flagship chipset-du-jour, the Snapdragon 8 Gen 1. While Motorola appears to have addressed  some of our complaints about that mid-range device, this is also a much more expensive phone at $999, setting the bar that much higher for its feature set.
 The Edge Plus checks plenty of boxes for a flagship phone in 2022: there’s a big 6.7-inch 1080p OLED with up to 144Hz refresh rate, sub-6GHz 5G including C-band (a Verizon version of the phone will have mmWave, the unlocked model won’t), a 4,800mAh battery, 8GB or 12GB of RAM, fast 30W wired charging, and 15W wireless charging. Motorola even includes a 30W charger in the box, a quaint vestige of years past.
  Motorola is also offering a new Smart Stylus, sold separately as a bundle with a case designed to hold it. It’s an active stylus in the vein of Samsung’s S Pen that can do more than the simple passive stylus sold with phones like Motorola’s Moto G Stylus. Like the S Pen, it’s sensitive to pressure and can be used as a remote control for certain actions without touching the pen to the screen. Motorola says it will announce pricing and availability for the stylus and case “in the coming weeks.”
 There are two 50-megapixel cameras on the Edge Plus’ rear panel: an f/1.8 standard wide with optical image stabilization and an f/2.2 ultrawide. The main camera should offer improved autofocus, which was a weak spot for the 2021 Edge. The third lens on the phone’s rear triple-camera array is a 2-megapixel depth sensor of questionable utility. On the front, there’s a 60-megapixel selfie camera.
  However, there are a few spots on the spec sheet that don’t look as rosy, especially for a $1,000 phone. Weather resistance is only rated at IP52, meaning there’s some protection against water spray, but it can’t be fully submerged like the IP68 devices in this class.
 There’s glass on the front and back of the phone, which is good, but it’s the older Gorilla Glass 3 and 5, respectively. Both the Pixel 6 and Samsung S22 Plus use newer Gorilla Glass Victus (Victus  Plus in the S22’s case) on the front to protect from both scratches and drops. The Edge Plus is also built with a polycarbonate plastic exterior frame rather than aluminum. Motorola says it will support the Edge Plus with three years of security updates, which also falls short of the best support policies among Android phones.
 If nothing else, the Edge Plus is another high-end Android option in the US, where  we have precious few devices to choose from. The Edge Plus is available unlocked for $100 off for a “limited time.” It will be offered from Verizon and other carriers in the coming months, according to Motorola.
title: 摩托罗拉推出售价999美元的Edge Plus，配备6.7英寸显示屏、高达144Hz的刷新率、可选智能触控笔和30W有线充电，将于未来几个月推出
title_en: Motorola debuts $999 Edge Plus with a 6.7" display and up to a 144Hz refresh rate, optional Smart Stylus, and 30W wired charging, available in the coming months

在准备让员工重返办公室之际，谷歌正在取消一些与新冠病毒相关的员工授权，并恢复总部的福利。
谷歌房地产和WorksServices VP David Radcliffe本周给旧金山湾地区的员工写了一封电子邮件，解释说公司正在放宽疫苗、测试、社会距离和口罩的一些规定。另一方面，谷歌发言人告诉CNBC，该公司已经改变了做法，不会要求美国工人以接种疫苗作为就业条件，但拒绝提供进一步细节。
拉德克利夫&#39；s note还表示，按摩和进入办公室非正式空间等额外福利也将恢复。过去，谷歌能够通过有趣的办公设施吸引人才，但其中许多设施在疫情期间被暂停。
这些举措出台之际，该公司正准备要求大多数员工每周至少三天进入实体办公室，为期34天；混合型&#34；工作模式，而其他一些湾区科技公司则提供完全远程工作选项。在2019冠状病毒疾病的浪潮中，谷歌不得不推迟其办公室的复出次数，而另一次激增可能再次改变这些最新计划。
自疫情爆发之初，谷歌和其他科技公司首次将员工遣送回家以来，已经过去了近两年。从那时起，劳动力市场收紧，员工抱怨缺少福利，工人们表达了继续远程工作的愿望。
拉德克利夫&#39；美国的报告称，湾区办公室正在取消1月份实施的检测要求，该要求甚至适用于接种疫苗的员工。
此外，谷歌正在大多数地区取消社交距离要求和接种疫苗员工的口罩，并指出仍需要口罩的少数城市可能很快就会取消口罩。
谷歌发言人洛拉·李·埃里克森（Lora Lee Erickson）表示，谷歌也在改变要求美国员工就业接种疫苗的做法。11月，CNBC报道称，该公司告诉员工必须遵守疫苗政策，否则他们&#39；他们将面临工资损失，最终失去工作。
埃里克森告诉CNBC，公司在取消了1月18日规定的员工接种疫苗或获得豁免批准的最后期限后，上个月取消了雇佣要求。她拒绝提供有关该政策或撤销原因的进一步细节，但指出，员工仍需要接种疫苗或获得豁免才能进入办公室。
"我们&#39；继续实施2019冠状病毒疾病疫苗接种政策，要求任何人进入我们的网站，因为它和39；这是我们保持员工安全和服务运行的最重要方式之一；埃里克森指出。
获准进入办公室的未接种疫苗的员工仍需遵守附加协议，包括测试和戴口罩，Radcliffe和#39；这张便条上写着。
拉德克利夫还写道，该公司正在重新开放设施，如健身中心，无需预约和按摩；恢复全面的穿梭服务；增加更多吃免费早餐和午餐的地方；开幕式&#34；所有非正式空间&#34；比如休息室、游戏室、音乐室和按摩椅。
"我们&#39；我们正处于旅程的开始，因此办公室体验将与新冠肺炎之前的感觉非常相似；拉德克利夫说"我们&#39；重新设计和试点选项，以支持新的合作方式，我们&#39；我们将收集见解、数据和反馈，帮助我们边走边学习"
"我们&#39；我们为欢迎有机会进入办公室的员工提供在我们安全的情况下尽可能做到这一点的选择，同时允许那些没有进入办公室的员工&#39；我还没有准备好继续在家工作；埃里克森告诉CNBC"根据湾区的现状，我们&#39；我们很高兴我们的员工现在能够进入更多的现场空间和服务，工作和与同事联系"
旧金山湾地区是谷歌办公楼的最高聚集地，数十个建筑遍布该地区的几个城市，包括其山景总部。拉德克利夫说，过去一周，该地区30%的谷歌员工来到了办公室，但返回仍然是自愿的。
自从去年1月10日在omicron激增之际取消预期以来，该公司尚未确定强制退货的新日期。但是在标题为&#34；接下来会发生什么" 拉德克利夫指出，如果情况继续改善，公司正准备开始为期30天的过渡期，过渡到混合工作周。他说他的团队正在计划&#34；庆祝活动&#34；欢迎员工回来。
note_en:
Google is dropping some Covid-related mandates for employees and restoring perks back to its headquarters as it prepares to bring workers back to the office.
 Google Real Estate and Workplace Services VP David Radcliffe wrote an email to San Francisco Bay Area employees this week explaining that the company is relaxing some rules around vaccines, testing, social distancing and masks. Separately, a Google spokesperson told CNBC that the company has reversed course and will not require vaccinations as a condition of employment for U.S. workers, but declined to offer further details.
  Radcliffe's note also said that perks such as massages and access to informal spaces in the office will be returning. In the past, Google has been able to attract talent with fun office amenities, but many of those were suspended during the pandemic.
 The moves come as the company prepares to require most employees to come into physical offices at least three days a week for a  "hybrid" work model, while some other Bay Area tech companies are offering fully remote work options. Google has had to delay its office return several times amid various Covid-19 variant surges, and another surge could change these latest plans once again.
 It has almost been two years since Google and other tech companies first sent their  employees home at the start of the pandemic. Since then, the labor market has tightened, employees have grumbled about missing  perks, and workers have voiced their desire to remain working remotely.
  Radcliffe's note says Bay Area offices are lifting the testing  requirement imposed in January, which applied even to vaccinated employees.
 In addition, Google is dropping its social distancing requirement and masks for vaccinated employees in most areas, noting that the few cities still requiring masks are likely to drop them soon.
  Google is also reversing course on requiring vaccination for employment for U.S. employees, according to Google spokesperson Lora Lee Erickson. In November, CNBC reported the company told employees that they must comply with vaccine policies or they'd face  loss of pay and eventually loss of employment.
 Erickson told CNBC the company dropped the requirement for employment last month after removing the Jan. 18 deadline it had set for employees to either get vaccinated or get exemption approval. She declined to provide further details on the policy or the reasons for the reversal, but noted that employees would still need to be vaccinated or have an exemption in order to come into the office.
 "We're continuing to implement our vaccination policy requiring COVID-19 vaccinations or approved accommodations for any individuals accessing our sites, because it's one of the most important ways we can keep our workforce safe and keep our services running," Erickson noted.
 Unvaccinated employees who are approved to enter offices will still need to follow additional protocols, including testing and wearing a mask, Radcliffe's note stated.
  Radcliffe also wrote that the company is reopening amenities such as fitness centers without appointment and massages; restoring full shuttle service; adding more places to eat free breakfast and lunch; and opening "all informal spaces" such as lounges, game rooms, music rooms and massage chairs.
 "We're at the beginning of a journey, so the office experience will feel pretty similar to what it was like pre-Covid," Radcliffe said. "We're designing and piloting options to support new ways of working together and we'll gather insights, data and feedback to help us learn as we go."
  "We're giving employees who welcome the chance to come into the office the option to do that wherever we safely can, while allowing those who aren't ready to keep working from home," Erickson told CNBC. "Based on current conditions in the Bay Area, we're pleased that our employees who choose to come in now have the ability to access more onsite spaces and services to work and connect with colleagues."
 The San Francisco Bay Area has the highest concentration of Google offices, with dozens of buildings across several cities in the region, including its Mountain View headquarters. Radcliffe said 30% of Google employees in the area came into the office over the past week, but the return remains voluntary.
 The company has still not determined a new date for the mandated return since it last  pushed off its Jan. 10 expectation amid the omicron surge. But under a line titled "What Happens Next?" Radcliffe notes that it is preparing to begin its 30-day transition period to the hybrid work week if conditions continue to improve. He said his team is planning "celebrations" to welcome back employees.

这轮融资总额达到3500万美元，由Craft Ventures牵头，Teamworthy Ventures、Altai Ventures、Eigen Ventures、Bling、Concimate和Touchdown Ventures以及当前和以前的M&amp；一位高管。
该公司打算利用这些资金加速其业务范围。Grata由首席执行官安德鲁·博茨科斯基（Andrew Bocskocksky）和首席运营官内文·拉吉（Nevin Raj）共同创立，全面报道私营公司，提供有关数百万公司的信息和微妙的背景见解。它的产品结合了一个扫描数十亿个网站的网络数据采集引擎，以及专有的机器学习（ML）和自然语言处理（NLP）模型，这些模型直观地将非结构化信息处理为对私营公司的细致的、上下文相关的见解。
note_en:
Grata, a New York-based private company intelligence engine provider, raised $25M in Series A funding.
  The round, which brings total funding to $35m, was led by Craft Ventures with participation from Teamworthy Ventures, Altai Ventures, Eigen Ventures, Bling, Accomplice, and Touchdown Ventures as well as current and former M&A executives.
 The company intends to use the funds to accelerate its business reach.  Co-founded by CEO Andrew Bocskocsky and COO Nevin Raj, Grata provides comprehensive coverage of private companies, featuring information and nuanced contextual insights on millions of companies. Its product combines a web data acquisition engine that scans billions of websites and proprietary machine learning (ML) and natural language processing (NLP) models that intuitively process unstructured information into nuanced, contextual insights on private companies.

Meta正在测试一个人工智能系统，该系统可以让人们通过描述虚拟世界来构建虚拟世界的一部分，首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）在今天的一次现场活动上展示了一个原型。这个名为“建筑机器人”的概念证明，最终可能会吸引更多人进入Meta的地平线“metaverse”虚拟现实体验。它还可以推动创造人工智能技术，为机器生成的艺术提供动力。
在一段预先录制的演示视频中，扎克伯格向观众介绍了使用Builder Bot创建虚拟空间的过程，从“让我们去海滩”这样的命令开始，它会提示Bot创建一个由他周围的沙子和水组成的卡通3D景观。（扎克伯格将其描述为“所有人工智能产生的”。）后来的命令范围很广，比如建造一座岛屿，也有极为具体的要求，比如添加高积云，还有一个笑话是自嘲自己的水翼艇模型。它们还包括播放像“热带音乐”这样的音效，扎克伯格认为这是来自Builder Bot创建的一个boombox，尽管它也可能是一般的背景音频。视频没有具体说明Builder Bot是利用有限的人工创建模型库，还是人工智能在生成设计中发挥了作用。
几个人工智能项目已经演示了基于文本描述的图像生成，包括OpenAI的DALL-E、Nvidia的GauGAN2和VQGAN+CLIP，以及更易访问的应用程序，如Wombo的Dream。但这些著名的项目涉及创建没有交互组件的二维图像（有时非常超现实），尽管一些研究人员正在研究三维对象生成。
正如Meta所描述并在演示中所示，Builder Bot似乎正在使用语音输入来添加用户可以四处走动的3D对象，Meta的目标是实现更宏大的交互。扎克伯格在活动主旨演讲中承诺：“你将能够创造微妙的世界，用自己的声音探索并与他人分享经验。”。Meta在活动期间发布了其他几项人工智能公告，包括通用语言翻译计划、对话人工智能系统的新版本，以及为没有大型书面数据集的语言构建新翻译模型的倡议。
扎克伯格承认，复杂的互动性，包括许多虚拟现实用户认为理所当然的可用虚拟对象，构成了重大挑战。如果用户要求具有攻击性的内容，或者人工智能的训练再现了人类对世界的偏见和刻板印象，人工智能一代可能会带来独特的节制问题。我们不知道当前系统的局限性。所以现在，你不应该期待在Meta的社交虚拟现实平台上出现Builder Bot，但你可以领略Meta对其人工智能未来的计划。
note_en:
Meta is testing an artificial intelligence system that lets people build parts of virtual worlds by describing them, and CEO Mark Zuckerberg showed off a prototype at a live event today. Proof of the concept, called Builder Bot, could eventually draw more people into Meta’s Horizon “metaverse” virtual reality experiences. It could also advance creative AI tech that powers machine-generated art.
 In a prerecorded demo video, Zuckerberg walked viewers through the process of making a virtual space with Builder Bot, starting with commands like “let’s go to the beach,” which prompts the bot to create a cartoonish 3D landscape of sand and water around him. (Zuckerberg describes this as “all AI-generated.”) Later commands range from broad demands like creating an island to extremely specific requests like adding  altocumulus clouds and — in a joke  poking fun at himself — a model of a hydrofoil. They also include playing sound effects like “tropical music,” which Zuckerberg suggests is coming from a boombox that Builder Bot created, although it could also have been general background audio. The video doesn’t specify whether Builder Bot draws on a limited library of human-created models or if the AI plays a role in generating the designs.
  Several AI projects have demonstrated image generation based on text descriptions, including  OpenAI’s DALL-E,  Nvidia’s GauGAN2, and  VQGAN+CLIP, as well as more accessible applications  like Dream by Wombo. But these well-known projects involve creating 2D images (sometimes very surreal ones) without interactive components, although some researchers are working on  3D object generation.
 As described by Meta and shown in the demo, Builder Bot appears to be using voice input to add 3D objects that users can walk around, and Meta is aiming for more ambitious interactions. “You’ll be able to create nuanced worlds to explore and share experiences with others with just your voice,” Zuckerberg promised during the event keynote. Meta made several other AI announcements during the event, including plans for a  universal language translator, a new version of a conversational AI system, and an initiative to build new translation models for languages without large written data sets.
 Zuckerberg acknowledged that sophisticated interactivity, including the kinds of usable virtual objects many VR users take for granted, poses major challenges. AI generation can pose unique moderation problems if users ask for offensive content or the AI’s training reproduces  human biases and stereotypes about the world. And we don’t know the limits of the current system. So for now, you shouldn’t expect to see Builder Bot pop up in  Meta’s social VR platform — but you can get a taste of Meta’s plans for its AI future.
title: 马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）展示了一个由人工智能生成的语音控制虚拟现实空间的概念，并计划开发一个通用语言翻译器，以及元直播流中的更多内容
title_en: Mark Zuckerberg showcases a concept for voice-controlled VR space generated by AI, plans for a universal language translator, and more in a Meta livestream

note:
Meta公司（前身为Facebook）已将其长期战略从社交媒体应用转移到metaverse上。metaverse是一个虚拟世界，戴着增强型/虚拟现实耳机的人可以与对方的头像交谈、玩游戏、举行会议，以及以其他方式参与社交活动。
这就产生了很多问题，比如这对一家专注于社交媒体近20年的公司意味着什么，Meta是否能够实现其构建metaverse未来的新目标，以及每天使用Meta产品的数十亿人的未来会是什么样子。周三，Meta首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）在一次关于该公司人工智能最新发展的主题演讲中透露了一些答案。
Meta的主要目标之一是开发高级语音助手人工智能技术——想想Alexa或Siri，但更智能——该公司计划在其AR/VR产品中使用该技术，比如其Quest headset（以前的Oculus）、Portal smart display和Ray Ban智能眼镜。
扎克伯格说：“你在元宇宙中的体验已经超出了今天的可能。”。“这将需要在一系列领域取得进步，从新的硬件设备到用于构建和探索世界的软件。而解锁这些进步的关键是人工智能。”
此次演讲正值该公司历史上最具挑战性的时刻之一。Meta的股价出现了历史性的下跌，其广告模式因苹果的移动隐私变化而发生了动摇，它还面临着迫在眉睫的政治监管威胁。
因此，该公司展望未来是有道理的，Meta希望在未来推出复杂的语言处理人工智能。
据Meta发言人表示，这是Meta首次举办专门展示其人工智能发展的活动。尽管如此，该公司承认这种人工智能仍在开发中，尚未得到广泛应用。示威活动是探索性的；Meta周三的演示视频底部包含免责声明，其中许多图片和示例仅用于说明目的，而不是实际产品。此外：元宇宙中的化身仍然没有腿。
不过，如果Meta正在推动其世界级的计算机科学研究人员开发这些工具，它很有可能会成功。如果完全实现，这些技术可能会改变我们在现实生活和虚拟现实中的沟通方式。这些发展也对如何存储和共享从AI驱动的可穿戴设备收集的更多个人数据提出了重大隐私担忧。
关于Meta如何使用新的人工智能模型构建语音助手，以及人工智能超级强大的metaverse带来的隐私和道德问题，这里有几点需要了解。
周三，很明显，Meta将语音助手视为metaverse的一个关键部分，并且它知道其语音助手需要比我们现在拥有的更加健谈。例如，大多数语音助手都能轻松回答“今天天气怎么样？”但如果你问一个后续问题，比如“天气比上周热吗？”语音助手可能会被难倒。
Meta希望它的语音助手能够更好地在对话中提取上下文线索，以及它可以收集的关于我们身体的其他数据点，比如我们的凝视、面部表情和手势。
扎克伯格周三表示：“为了支持真实世界的创造和探索，我们需要在智能助手方面超越目前的技术水平。”。
虽然Meta的主要技术竞争对手亚马逊、苹果和谷歌已经有了流行的语音助手产品，无论是在移动设备上还是像Alexa这样的独立硬件上，Meta都没有（除了Ray Bans、Oculus和门户设备上的一些有限的语音命令功能）。
扎克伯格说：“当我们戴上眼镜时，这将是人工智能系统第一次能够真正从我们的角度看世界——看到我们看到的，听到我们听到的，等等。”。“因此，我们对人工智能系统的能力和期望将高得多。”
为了满足这些期望，该公司表示，它正在开发一个名为CAIRaoke的项目，这是一种自学习人工智能神经模型（这是一种基于人脑生物网络的统计模型），为其语音助手提供动力。该模型使用“自我监督学习”，这意味着人工智能本质上可以自学，而不是像许多其他人工智能模型那样在大型数据集上进行训练。
Meta的人工智能研究常务董事Joëlle Pineau告诉Recode：“以前，所有的积木都是单独建造的，然后你可以把它们粘在一起。”。“随着我们转向自我监督学习，我们有能力学习整个对话。”
作为这项技术如何应用的一个例子，扎克伯格以虚拟现实化身的形式演示了该公司正在开发的一个名为“BuilderBot”的工具，它可以让你说出你想在虚拟现实中看到的东西。例如，根据该公司的说法，说“我想看那边的一棵棕榈树”可以让人工智能生成的棕榈树在你想看的地方弹出，具体取决于你所说的话、你的目光、你的控制器/手以及一般的上下文意识。
Meta仍然需要做更多的研究才能做到这一点，它正在研究所谓的“以自我为中心的感知”，即从第一人称的角度来理解世界，以构建这一点。目前，该公司正在其门户智能显示屏上测试该模型的技术。
最终，该公司还希望能够捕捉语音以外的输入——比如用户的动作、位置和肢体语言，以构建更智能的虚拟助手，预测用户的需求。
隐私问题和失败一直困扰着Meta和其他大型科技公司，因为它们的商业模式建立在收集用户数据的基础上：我们的浏览历史、兴趣、个人通信等等。
隐私专家说，使用AR/VR，这些担忧甚至更大，因为它可以跟踪更敏感的数据，比如我们的眼动、面部表情和肢体语言。
一些AR/VR和AI伦理学家担心这些数据输入会变成什么样的个人数据，AI可以用这些输入做出什么样的预测，以及这些数据将如何共享。
XR安全倡议的创始人卡维亚·珀尔曼（Kavya Pearlman）说：“眼球跟踪数据，凝视数据，从字面上来说，能够量化你是感觉到性唤起的刺激还是充满爱的凝视——所有这些都是值得关注的。”XR安全倡议是一家倡导虚拟现实等技术道德发展的非营利组织。“谁有权访问这些数据？他们在用这些数据做什么？”
目前，这些问题的答案还不完全清楚，尽管Meta表示它致力于解决这些问题。
扎克伯格表示，该公司正在与人权、民权和隐私专家合作，建立“基于公平、尊重和人类尊严的系统”
但考虑到该公司侵犯隐私的记录，一些技术伦理学家持怀疑态度。
“从纯科学的角度来看，我真的很兴奋。但因为它是元的，我很害怕，”珀尔曼说。
针对人们对metaverse隐私的担忧，Meta的Pineau表示，通过让用户控制他们共享的数据，该公司可以帮助缓解人们的担忧。
她说：“当人们从中获得价值时，他们愿意分享信息。因此，如果你看一看，自主、控制和透明的概念才是真正允许用户对他们的数据如何使用拥有更多控制权的地方。”。
除了隐私问题，一些Meta AR/VR用户担心，如果人工智能驱动的metaverse起飞，它可能无法供所有人访问，也不安全。已经有一些女性抱怨在metaverse中遇到性骚扰，比如Meta社交VR应用程序Horizon Worlds的一名测试人员报告说，其他用户几乎在摸索。此后，Meta在头像周围建立了一个4英尺高的虚拟安全泡泡，以帮助避免“不必要的互动”
如果Meta实现了使用人工智能使其AR/VR环境在我们的日常生活中更具沉浸感和无缝性的目标，那么围绕可访问性、安全性和歧视的更多问题可能会浮出水面。尽管Facebook表示从一开始就在考虑这些问题，但它在其他产品方面的业绩并不令人放心。
数百万人转向Vox了解新闻中发生的事情。我们的使命从未像现在这样重要：通过理解赋予权力。读者的经济贡献是支持我们资源密集型工作的关键部分，有助于我们让所有人都能自由地报道新闻。请考虑今天为VoX做贡献。
note_en:
Meta, the company formerly known as Facebook, has shifted its long-term strategy away from its social media apps to focus on the metaverse, a virtual world where people wearing augmented/virtual reality headsets can talk to each others’ avatars, play games, hold meetings, and otherwise engage in social activities.
 That’s created a lot of questions, such as what this means for a company that has been focused on social media for nearly two decades, whether Meta will be able to achieve its new goal of building a metaverse future, and what that future will look like for the billions of people who use Meta’s products every day. On Wednesday, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg revealed some answers during a keynote speech about the company’s latest developments in AI.
 One of Meta’s main goals is to develop advanced voice assistant AI technology — think Alexa or Siri, but smarter — that the company plans to use in its AR/VR products, like its Quest headset (formerly Oculus), Portal smart display, and  Ray-Ban smart glasses.
 “The kinds of experiences you’ll have in the metaverse are beyond what’s possible today,” said Zuckerberg. “That’s going to require advances across a whole range of areas, from new hardware devices to software for building and exploring worlds. And the key to unlocking a lot of these advances is AI.”
 The presentation comes during one of the most challenging moments in the company’s history.  Meta’s share prices have taken a historic dip, its  advertising model has been shaken up by Apple’s mobile privacy changes, and it faces the looming threat of  political regulation.
 So it makes sense that the company is looking to the future, in which Meta hopes to roll out sophisticated language-processing AI.
   It’s the first time Meta has had an event solely dedicated to showcasing its AI developments, according to a Meta spokesperson. That being said, the company admits this AI is still in development and not widely used yet. The demonstrations are exploratory; Meta’s demo videos on Wednesday included disclaimers at the bottom that many of the images and examples are strictly for illustrative purposes and not actual products. Also: Avatars in the metaverse still don’t have legs.
 If Meta is pushing its world-class computer science researchers to develop these tools, though, there’s a good chance it will succeed. And if fully realized, these technologies could change how we communicate, both in real life and in virtual reality. These developments also present significant privacy concerns about how more personal data collected from AI-powered wearable devices is stored and shared.
 Here are a few things to know about how Meta is building out a voice assistant using new AI models, as well the privacy and ethical concerns an AI-superpowered metaverse raises.
  On Wednesday, it became clear that Meta sees voice assistants as a key part of the metaverse, and it knows that its voice assistant needs to be more conversational than what we have now. For example, most voice assistants can easily answer the question, “What’s the weather today?” But if you ask a follow-up question, such as, “Is it hotter than it was last week?” the voice assistant will likely be stumped.
 Meta wants its voice assistant to be better at picking up contextual clues in conversations, along with other data points that it can collect about our physical body, like our gaze, facial expressions, and hand gestures.
 “To support true world creation and exploration, we need to advance beyond the current state of the art for smart assistants,” said Zuckerberg on Wednesday.
 While Meta’s Big Tech competitors — Amazon, Apple, and Google — already have popular voice assistant products, either on mobile or as standalone hardware like Alexa, Meta doesn’t (aside from some limited voice command functionality on its Ray-Bans, Oculus, and Portal devices).
 “When we have glasses on our faces, that will be the first time an AI system will be able to really see the world from our perspective — see what we see, hear what we hear, and more,” said Zuckerberg. “So the ability and expectation we have for AI systems will be much higher.”
 To meet those expectations, the company says it’s been developing a project called CAIRaoke, a self-learning AI neural model (that’s a statistical model based on biological networks in the human brain) to power its voice assistant. This model uses “self-supervised learning,” meaning that rather than being trained on large datasets the way many other AI models are, the AI can essentially teach itself.
 “Before, all the blocks were built separately, and then you sort of glued them together,” Meta’s managing director of AI research, Joëlle Pineau, told Recode. “As we move to self-supervised learning, we have the ability to learn the whole conversation.”
 As one example of how this technology can be applied, Zuckerberg — in virtual reality avatar form — demoed a tool the company is working on called “BuilderBot” that allows you to speak out what you want to see in your virtual reality. For instance, saying “I want to see a palm tree over there” could make an AI-generated palm tree pop up where you want, based on what you say, your gaze, your controllers/hands, and general contextual awareness, according to the company.
 Meta still needs to do more research for this to be possible, and it’s studying what’s called “egocentric perception,” which is about understanding worlds from a first-person perspective, to build this out. Currently, it’s testing the technology from the model in its Portal smart displays.
 Eventually, the company also hopes to be able to capture inputs beyond speech — like a user’s movement, position, and body language, to build even smarter virtual assistants that can anticipate what users want.
  Privacy concerns and  failures have haunted Meta and other big tech companies because their business models are built around collecting users’ data: our browsing histories, interests, personal communications, and more.
 Those concerns are even greater, privacy experts say, with AR/VR, because it can track even more sensitive data, like our eye movements, facial expressions, and body language.
 Some AR/VR and AI ethicists are worried about just how personal these data inputs can become, what kind of predictions AI can make with those inputs, and how that data will be shared.
 “Eye-tracking data, gaze data, literally being able to quantify whether you’re feeling stimuli off of sexual arousal or a loving gaze — all of that is concerning,” said Kavya Pearlman, founder of the XR Safety Initiative, a nonprofit that advocates for the ethical development of technologies like VR. “Who has access to this data? What are they doing with this data?”
 For now, the answers to those questions aren’t entirely clear, although Meta is saying it’s committed to addressing such concerns.
 Zuckerberg said that the company is working with human rights, civil rights, and privacy experts to build “systems grounded in fairness, respect, and human dignity.”
 But given the company’s  track record of privacy breaches, some technology ethicists are skeptical.
 “From a purely scientific perspective, I’m really excited. But because it is Meta, I’m scared,” said Pearlman.
 In response to people’s concerns about privacy in the metaverse, Meta’s Pineau said that by giving users control over what data they share, the company can help alleviate people’s worries.
 “People are willing to share information when there’s value that they derive out of that. And so if you look at it, the notion of autonomy, control, and transparency is what really allows the users to have more control over how their data is used,” she said.
 Aside from privacy concerns, some Meta AR/VR users worry that if an AI-powered metaverse takes off, it may not be accessible to and safe for everyone. Already, some women have complained about encountering sexual harassment in the metaverse, such as when a beta tester of Meta’s social VR app Horizon Worlds reported being  virtually groped by other users. Meta has since instituted what amounts to a 4-foot  virtual safety bubble around avatars to help avoid “unwanted interactions.”
 If Meta reaches its goal of using AI to make its AR/VR environments even more immersive and seamless in our daily lives, more problems around accessibility, safety, and discrimination are likely to surface. And though Facebook says it’s thinking about these concerns at the outset, its track record with its other products isn’t reassuring.
  Millions turn to Vox to understand what’s happening in the news. Our mission has never been more vital than it is in this moment: to empower through understanding. Financial contributions from our readers are a critical part of supporting our resource-intensive work and help us keep our journalism free for all. title_en: Meta says it has been developing CAIRaoke, a self-supervised learning AI neural model used to power a voice assistant for the company's AR/VR products

该公司公布的第四季度业绩超过了预期。
然而，Etsy对第一季度的指导很弱，将其归咎于与去年同期的艰难比较，当时这家数字零售商的在线订单出现了与大流行相关的增长。
周四，Etsy公布第四季度业绩好于预期后，该公司股价在延长交易期内上涨了18%。股票在常规交易中已经上涨了10%，因为股票在俄罗斯消息第一次暴跌后在尾盘反弹&#39；美国入侵乌克兰。
Etsy表示，截至第四季度，该平台上有9630万活跃买家，略高于分析师#39；预计9560万。
本季度收入同比增长放缓至16%。Etsy的销售增长在2020年达到100%，但在最近几个季度有所放缓。
这家数字零售商表示，预计第一季度收入将在5.65亿美元至5.9亿美元之间，而华尔街预计收入为6.3亿美元。本季度的商品销售总额预计在32亿至34亿美元之间，低于普遍估计的35亿美元。
但投资者似乎并不为中等预期所困扰，而是关注第四季度的盈利和销售业绩。
Etsy首席财务官雷切尔·格拉泽（Rachel Glaser）将第一季度GMS前景疲弱归咎于与去年同期的艰难对比，当时该公司看到了与大流行相关的订单增长，以及与政府刺激相关的支出增加。
像Etsy、Shopify、eBay和Wayfair这样的电子商务公司在新冠病毒疫情最严重的几个月都经历了收入提升。在封锁期间，许多消费者为了避免病毒传播而限制了去商店的行程，并转向在线零售商购买必要和非必要的商品。
格拉泽说，她相信Etsy能够在疫情后的世界里继续扩大业务。Etsy经营着一个以手工制作和个性化商品闻名的在线市场。
"即使没有刺激检查和锁定的显著尾声，我们的第一季度2022指导反映了我们的预期，我们将保持2021的所有收益-表明我们对过去两年和39年的耐久性的信念；增长&#34；格拉泽说"此外，假设宏观经济状况稳定，鉴于上半年的比较更具挑战性，我们目前预计2022年上半年GMS同比增长较低，下半年GMS同比增长较高"
note_en:
The company reported fourth-quarter results that topped estimates on the top and the bottom line.
 However, Etsy gave weak guidance for the first quarter, blaming it on tough comparisons with the year-earlier period when the digital retailer had a pandemic-related boost in online orders.
 Shares of  Etsy climbed as much as 18% in extended trading Thursday after the company reported better-than-expected results for the fourth quarter. Shares had already climbed 10% during regular trading, as stocks staged a  late-day rally after first plunging on news of Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
    Etsy said it had 96.3 million active buyers on the platform as of the fourth quarter, a touch higher than analysts' projected 95.6 million.
 Revenue growth slowed to 16% year over year during the quarter. Etsy sales growth topped 100% in 2020, but  have decelerated in recent quarters.
 The digital retailer said it expects first-quarter revenue to come in between $565 million and $590 million, while Wall Street projected revenue of $630 million. Gross merchandise sales during the quarter are projected to be in the range of $3.2 billion to $3.4 billion, which is lower than consensus estimates of $3.5 billion.
 But investors appeared to be unfazed by the middling expectations, focusing instead on the fourth-quarter earnings and sales results.
 Etsy CFO Rachel Glaser blamed the weak first-quarter GMS outlook on tough comparisons with the year-earlier period when the company saw a pandemic-related boost in orders, as well as an increase in spending tied to government stimulus.
  E-commerce companies like Etsy,  Shopify,  eBay and  Wayfair have all experienced a revenue lift during the worst months of the coronavirus pandemic. Amid lockdowns, many consumers curbed trips to the store to avoid spreading the virus and turned to online retailers for essential and nonessential purchases.
 Glaser said she believes Etsy, which operates an online marketplace known for handmade and personalized goods, will be able to keep expanding its business in a post-pandemic world.
 "Even without the significant tail winds of stimulus checks and lockdowns, our first quarter 2022 guidance reflects our expectation that we will keep all of the gains made in 2021 — indicating our belief in the durability of the last two years' growth," Glaser said. "Furthermore, assuming stable macroeconomic conditions, we currently expect lower GMS growth year over year in the first half of 2022 and higher GMS growth in the second half, given the more challenging comparisons in the first half."

note_en:
China's Supreme Court  ruled Thursday that virtual asset transactions constitute "illegal fundraising," paving the way for judicial prosecution of the crypto industry.
 While the People's Bank of China and a host of other top-level agencies had  declared that crypto transactions are illegal fundraising back in September 2021, the court ruling formally designates them as a crime and determines related punishments.
 Thursday's ruling said that suspects will be prosecuted under Article 176 of  China's criminal law, which stipulates prison sentences between three and 10 years and fines between RMB 50,000 (US$7,900) and RMB 500,000 ($79,000) for crimes involving large sums of money. Less serious crimes will be prosecuted with under three years of prison and RMB 20,000 ($3,160) to RMB 200,000 ($31,600) in fines, according to the criminal law.
  Chinese provincial authorities continue to crack down on the industry. The eastern province of Zhejiang announced increased electricity tariffs on Wednesday for crypto mining, joining Hainan and Inner Mongolia.

note_en:
Feb 24 (Reuters) - Insight Partners said on Thursday it has raised $20 billion for its twelfth flagship fund as the New York-based private equity and venture capital firm looks to ramp up investments in technology and software companies.
 The sum is the firm's largest fundraise to date and is more than twice the size of its previous flagship fund, which closed at $9.5 billion in April 2020.
 Insight Partners, which now has more than $90 billion in regulatory assets under management, has also closed a co-invest fund that will participate in certain investments alongside the flagship fund.
  The fundraise comes after a banner 2021 for U.S. private equity, with dealmaking and exit activity posting its fastest pace in at least two decades, according to data from Pitchbook.
 High growth, technology companies drove much of that activity as demand for their services soared and valuations boomed. Insight Partners also invested more than $2 billion in European startups last year.
 But fears of looming U.S. interest rate hikes to tame unruly inflation and geopolitical worries have weighed on technology stocks this year.
  "Software as a basic model has been extremely resilient. We don't see any particular worries and we aim to be consistent in backing high-growth software companies," said Managing Director Teddie Wardi.
 Founded in 1995, Insight Partners has over 600 companies in its portfolio - including Canadian e-commerce giant Shopify Inc  (SHOP.TO), , German food-delivery group Delivery Hero SE  (DHER.DE) and social media giant Twitter Inc  (TWTR.N). More than 55 of those companies have gone public through initial public offerings, the company says.

title_en: Insight Partners raises $20B for its 12th fund, its largest fundraise yet and more than double its flagship $9.5B fund in April 2020; the firm now manages $90B+

几家大型在线出版商正在采取措施，放弃Alphabet GOOG 3.99%的谷歌（Google）推出的一个项目，该项目被宣传为优化读者移动浏览体验的一种方式，称其产生的广告收入较少。
包括Vox Media LLC、BuzzFeed BZFD（6.67%复杂网络）和Bustle母公司BDG在内的公司表示，他们已经开始测试或正在考虑使用自己版本的移动优化文章页面，而不是使用加速移动页面框架构建，这是谷歌在2015年推出的，并得到了一个开源工作组的支持。《华盛顿邮报》更进一步，在去年夏天放弃了AMP。
退出AMP的可能性将使媒体公司对谷歌的依赖程度略有降低，谷歌在数字广告领域的主导地位已使其与出版商的关系紧张，并在2020年12月的一场指控反竞争行为的州检察长诉讼中被提及。
最近一个未经修改的诉讼版本声称，托管在谷歌服务器上的AMP页面是专门设计的，目的是让广告空间更难在谷歌广告交易所以外的平台上拍卖。它还声称，谷歌制作的广告没有使用AMP加载，只是延迟了一秒钟。
谷歌当时的一位发言人表示，诉讼中有关AMP的说法是错误的，并表示其工程师设计该系统是为了更快地加载网页。
媒体高管曾表示，放弃AMP将让他们对自己的页面设计和广告格式有更多的控制权，并使他们更容易通过标题竞价系统在包括更多广告市场的拍卖中出售广告空间，最终提高广告空间的竞争和价格。
谷歌表示，出版商可以通过标题竞价出售AMP页面上的广告空间，但这种做法也有其缺点，包括使用更多数据，导致网页加载速度较慢。
“AMP仍然是一种有用的方式，即使是那些没有大型数字团队的网站，也能轻松地创造伟大的网络体验，”谷歌发言人说。
媒体高管和顾问表示，他们预计在大多数情况下，非AMP页面的广告收入至少比AMP页面多20%，而不会影响加载速度。
BDG的首席技术官泰勒·洛夫（Tyler Love）说：“你可以让自己的网页表现得非常好，而不必做出谷歌要求的妥协，让AMP发挥作用。”BDG拥有Bustle、Gawker、尼龙和W等品牌。
谷歌表示，它提供了自己的标题竞价替代方案，称之为公开竞价。
一些出版商表示，在谷歌去年调整了算法，将页面的加载速度、交互性和视觉稳定性纳入搜索排名后，他们开始减少AMP的努力。谷歌曾表示，新标准旨在改善用户的内容体验和出版商的搜索结果性能，而无需创建单独的AMP页面。当时，谷歌还表示，它将停止在其顶级故事轮播中只播放AMP内容。
“如果有平等的机会做AMP和非AMP页面，我们将专注于非AMP页面，”复杂网络首席执行官兼BuzzFeed首席运营官克里斯蒂安·贝斯勒（Christian Baesler）说。其出版物包括复杂的和唯一的收藏家。
《华盛顿邮报》产品总监戴夫·梅雷尔（Dave Merrell）表示，在谷歌推出新的技术标准后，新闻机构于去年夏天停止发布AMP版本的文章。另外两家主要新闻出版商，纽约时报和道琼斯；《华尔街日报》出版商、新闻集团旗下的公司拒绝置评。
Vox Media的首席营收官瑞安·保利（Ryan Pauley）说，除了增加广告收入之外，他预计非AMP页面将使付费网站更容易实现订阅增长。该公司的标题包括《Verge》、《Vox》和《纽约杂志》。谷歌表示，出版商可以采取措施限制读者在AMP和非AMP页面上可以看到的免费文章数量。
多年来，新闻出版商和谷歌之间的关系一直令人担忧，而且超出了数字广告的范畴。包括新闻集团（news Corp）在内的主要新闻机构的高管长期以来一直批评谷歌在其产品中使用新闻内容，而没有向提供新闻内容的出版商付费。谷歌在2020年表示，它将在三年内向出版商支付总额超过10亿美元的费用，以授权新闻内容。新闻集团是与谷歌达成内容许可协议的出版商之一。
谷歌在2015年推出AMP时表示，它将为数字出版商和读者创造更好的体验。媒体公司签约，创建了专门的文章页面，旨在更快地在手机上加载，消除一些讨厌的广告，并使内容更容易查找。谷歌当时表示，该项目将使“万维网再次伟大”
一些出版商表示，他们担心离开AMP可能会影响他们的流量。
“我唯一担心的是我们没有意识到的错失机会成本，”BDG的洛夫说。例如，他说，目前尚不清楚从长远来看，使用AMP是否会损害有机搜索，或者非AMP页面是否有同样的机会出现在谷歌的一个特殊部分，比如Android手机的发现功能。
谷歌拒绝就具体问题置评，并表示AMP从来都不是搜索结果中的排名信号。
note_en:
Several large online publishers are taking steps to abandon a program from  Alphabet     GOOG  3.99%  Google that was billed as a way to optimize readers’ mobile-browsing experience, saying it generates less advertising revenue.
  Companies including Vox Media LLC,  BuzzFeed    BZFD  -6.67%  Complex Networks and Bustle parent BDG said they have started testing or are considering using their own versions of mobile-optimized article pages, instead of building them using the Accelerated Mobile Pages framework, which Google introduced in 2015 and is supported by an open-source working group. The Washington Post has gone a step further, abandoning AMP last summer.
  A potential exit from AMP would make media companies slightly less reliant on Google, whose dominance in digital advertising has strained its relationship with publishers and been referenced in  a December 2020 lawsuit by state attorneys general alleging anticompetitive behavior.
  A recently unredacted version of the lawsuit alleged that AMP pages—which are hosted on Google’s servers—have been specifically designed to make it more difficult for ad space to be  auctioned on platforms other than Google’s ad exchange. It also alleged that Google made ads that didn’t use AMP load with a one-second delay.
      A Google spokesman at the time said the lawsuit’s claims about AMP were false and said its engineers designed the system to load webpages faster.
  Media executives have said dropping AMP would give them more control over their page designs and ad formats, and make it easier for them to sell ad space in auctions that include a greater number of ad marketplaces through a system known as header bidding, ultimately boosting competition and prices for their ad space.
  Google said it is possible for publishers to sell ad space on AMP pages through header bidding, but the practice has its downsides, including using more data and causing webpages to load more slowly.
  “AMP continues to be a helpful way for sites—even those without large digital teams—to easily create great web experiences,” a Google spokeswoman said.
  Media executives and consultants said they expect non-AMP pages to garner at least 20% more advertising revenue than AMP pages in most cases, without affecting loading speeds.
  “You can make your own webpages perform really well without making compromises Google requires to make AMP work,” said Tyler Love, chief technology officer at BDG, which owns brands such as Bustle, Gawker, Nylon and W.
    Google said it offers  its own alternative to header bidding, which it calls Open Bidding.
  Some publishers said they started winding down their AMP efforts after Google last year tweaked its algorithm so that pages’ loading speeds, interactivity and visual stability became factored into its search rankings. The new standards were designed to improve users’ content experience and publishers’ search-result performance, Google had said, without having to create separate AMP pages. At the time, Google also said it would stop featuring only AMP content in its top-stories carousel.
  “If there’s equal opportunity to do AMP and non-AMP pages, we’ll focus on non-AMP pages,” said Christian Baesler, chief executive of Complex Networks and chief operating officer of BuzzFeed. Its publications include Complex and Sole Collector.
  Washington Post director of product Dave Merrell said the news organization stopped publishing AMP versions of its articles last summer after Google rolled out its new technology standards. Two other major news publishers,  New York Times  and Dow Jones & Co.—the publisher of The Wall Street Journal and a unit of  News Corp —declined to comment.
  Ryan Pauley, the chief revenue officer of Vox Media—whose titles include The Verge, Vox and New York Magazine—said that beyond the increased ad revenue, he expects non-AMP pages to make it easier for paywalled websites to achieve subscription growth. Google said publishers can take steps to cap the number of free articles readers can see on AMP and non-AMP pages.
  The relationship between news publishers and Google has been fraught through the years—and goes beyond digital advertising. Executives at major news organizations, including News Corp, have long  criticized Google for using news content in its products without paying the publishers that provide it. Google in 2020 said it would  pay publishers a total of more than $1 billion for three years to license news content. News Corp is among the publishers that have reached content-licensing deals with Google.
  When  Google introduced AMP in 2015, the company said it would create a better experience for digital publishers and readers. Media companies signed on, creating special article pages that were meant to load more quickly on mobile phones, eliminate some pesky ads and make it easier to find content. Google said at the time the program would make the “World Wide Web great again.”
  Some publishers say they are wary that moving away from AMP could hurt their traffic.
  “The only thing I worry about is missed opportunity costs we’re not aware of,” BDG’s Mr. Love said. For instance, it is unclear whether having AMP would harm organic search in the long run, or whether a non-AMP page would have the same chance of appearing in a special Google section like the Discover feature for Android phones, he said.
  Google declined to comment on the specific concerns and said AMP has never been a ranking signal in search results.
title: Vox Media、Complex Networks和BDG正在考虑放弃谷歌的AMP，推出自己的手机优化文章；《华盛顿邮报》去年夏天放弃了AMP
title_en: Vox Media, Complex Networks, and BDG are considering ditching Google's AMP for their own mobile-optimized articles; The Washington Post dropped AMP last summer

这一轮的投资者包括加密市场庄家和联合生态系统合作伙伴GSR Ventures、Altium Capital Management、Calvary Fund和一些私人投资者，包括电影制片人和体育经纪人Happy Walters。

总部位于波多黎各的Aligned的产品线包括为以太坊兼容区块链（包括第2层网络）提供采矿、立桩和流动性供应的技术，以及著名的区块链和新兴架构。
股权证明区块链的增长增加了对押桩和验证器的需求。Aligned启用了链上流动性供应，并表示，迄今为止，它已支持在分散金融（DeFi）生态系统中部署15000 ETH（约4.405亿美元）。
该公司表示，它正在与Neptune DAO等客户合作，为初创的DeFi项目提供流动性，以便将流动性部署到他们的平台上。
卡萨特说：“让DeFi应用程序工作需要三个基本要素，我们提供所有要素：1.协议中的流动性/资金，2.发送交易并运行区块链的软件，以及3.运行该应用程序的计算机，在我们的案例中，是专为这种用途设计的专用硬件。”。“总而言之，这是一个‘全栈’基础设施，因为它以垂直集成的方式在这些层上工作和运行。”
最近几周，ypto基础设施公司已成为风投宠儿，Blockdaemon以32.5亿美元的估值筹集了2.07亿美元，而Alchemy以100亿美元的估值带来了2亿美元。

note_en:
Aligned, a Web 3 infrastructure provider founded by former ConsenSys Chief Strategy Officer Sam Cassatt, has emerged from stealth with a $34 million funding round at an undisclosed valuation.
 Investors in the round included crypto market maker and Aligned ecosystem partner GSR Ventures, Altium Capital Management, Calvary Fund and a number of private investors, including film producer and sports agent Happy Walters.
  “We are buying silicon, manufacturing and installing the custom hardware that we produce with that silicon, as well as expanding our HPC (high-performance computing) team,” Cassatt told CoinDesk in an email. “Most of this hardware will go into our primary data center and will be expanding to more soon.”
 The product line of Puerto Rico-based Aligned includes technology for mining, staking and liquidity provisioning for Ethereum-compatible blockchains, including layer 2 networks, as well as prominent blockchains and emerging architectures.
 The growth of  proof-of-stake blockchains has increased the demand for staking and validators. Aligned enables on-chain liquidity provisioning and said it has, to date, supported the deployment of 15,000 ETH (about $440.5 million) across the  decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem.
 The company says it’s working with clients like Neptune DAO that provide liquidity to upstart DeFi projects to deploy liquidity to their platforms.
 “You need three basic things to make a DeFi application work, and we provide all of them: 1. liquidity/money in the protocol, 2. software that sends the transactions and runs the blockchain, and 3. computers to run it on, in our case specialized hardware that is designed for this use,” Cassatt said. “Taken together, this is a ‘full-stack’ infrastructure since it works and operates at these layers in a vertically integrated way.”
 ypto infrastructure firms have become venture capital darlings in recent weeks with  Blockdaemon raising $207 million at a $3.25 billion valuation and  Alchemy bringing in $200 million at a $10 billion valuation.
 CORRECTION (Feb. 24 22:52): Due to a copy editing error the name of the company making the announcement was made incorrect. The company is Aligned, not Aligned Capital, which is a separate entity not connected to this announcement.

note:
Dbt Labs的联合创始人兼首席执行官特里斯坦·汉迪（Tristan Handy）在科技股1月份突然暴跌时，已经安排了一系列顶级交叉基金，以62亿美元的估值投资他的公司。随着后期风险投资者重新评估他们给出的高估值，汉迪将自己的目标下调了近三分之一。
这家总部位于费城的数据分析初创公司周四透露，作为2.22亿美元D轮融资的一部分，该公司已确定了42亿美元的估值。Altimeter是前一次融资的共同领导者，是主要投资者，现有的赞助者Amplify Partners、Andreessen Horowitz和红杉也参与了此次融资。新投资者包括Databricks、Snowflake和Salesforce Ventures的三位数据云领导者，以及Coatue、GIC、Iconiq和Tiger Global。
《福布斯》去年12月首次报道称，Dbt实验室的目标估值至少为60亿美元。Handy声称，它最终实现的较低估值与投资者信心的转变无关。Altimeter合伙人贾明·鲍尔（Jamin Ball）肯定道：“他们可以以任何想要的价格提高价格。”。“需求几乎是无限的。”
“投资者已经准备好了，”汉迪说。“我们关心的是另一组利益相关者：员工。”他指出，如果一家初创公司上市，员工可以从股票期权中套现，但随着科技股的下跌，现在估值过高可能会导致IPO时的派息降低。他补充说：“我们最不希望的是，我们的员工或我们希望在未来一年雇佣的300人感到，我们没有给他们充分参与上涨的机会。”。（低代码软件初创公司Retool最近也使用了类似的论点。）
Handy承认，这种思路可能听起来很奇怪，尤其是在硅谷，去年的大部分时间里，硅谷的估值都达到了新的高度。但总部位于费城，他的妻子是儿童医院的医生，Handy有自己的北极星。2016年，他在纽约市新兴的科技生态系统中苦苦寻找高管级的分析工作后，以一家小型软件咨询公司的身份创办了这家公司。“嗯，我们不搬家了，”汉迪回忆起妻子的回答。“所以，我必须为自己创造机会。”它名为Fishtown Analytics，是对当地蓝领社区的认可，它开始为当地公司提供数据处理支持。
在此过程中，Handy的团队构建了一个内部“数据构建工具”（“dbt”），以提高咨询工作的生产率。该工具通过管道将许多不同类型的原始数据从云端涌入，并将其民主化，以便用于分析。咨询客户很快就注意到了这一内部工具，Fishtown Analytics已经将其开源。到2019年，已有1000多家企业采用了该技术，大型企业开始使用Handy。“他们说，‘我们想用这个东西，但你是一家15人的公司。我不知道我们能把如此关键的数据基础设施交给这么小的公司。’”机会很明显：把免费工具变成这些公司愿意购买的软件。在那之前，汉迪一直在推动自己的业务，现在他求助于风险投资家，为扩大规模提供资金。
据Pitchbook称，Handy于2020年4月在安德烈森·霍洛维茨（Andreessen Horowitz）领导的一轮投票中拿到了他的第一张支票，这轮投票对该公司的估值为4800万美元。在疫情的推动下，企业加快了数字化转型的步伐。Handy的初创公司凭借其数据转换工具，将自己打造成云数据工作流程的关键组成部分。许多业内人士都将其称为市场的明确领导者（对于规模如此之大的初创公司来说，这是不同寻常的）。它变成了独角兽，一年后更名为Dbt实验室。即便如此，它的增长速度也超过了预期。去年6月，Handy宣布计划到2022年底将员工数量增加到250人；他的目标已经翻了一番。大约9000家公司使用这个开源工具，其中1800多家公司通过其托管的云服务向客户付费。HADY拒绝评论收入，只是说它在2021增长了6倍，但是有两个来源把它放在了两位数的数百万。
最新的注资将帮助Dbt实验室继续扩大规模，满足不断增长的需求，尤其是在国外（不到40%的用户位于北美）。现在，Handy正在寻求构建新的软件工具，即用于标准化业务指标的工具。他说：“如今，数据专业人士的经验还有很多需要改进的地方。”。“你不是在讨论上个月的业务表现，而是在讨论技术细节，比如我的收入数字与你的收入数字之间的差异。这是一种极大的时间浪费，而且往往会阻止人们甚至在一开始就尝试使用数据。”
这些功能可能是Handy将Dbt实验室从订阅产品发展为“构建基础层”的野心的关键，就像新的支持者Snowflake和Databricks一样。两家公司的高管称，他们的新投资是对Dbt实验室在生态系统中的持久力的“信心投票”。Altimeter’s Ball补充道，由于云需求的增长，一些基础设施提供商能够继续加速增长，即使年收入达到10亿美元。他说：“Dbt在很多方面都与雪花这样的人一样，正处于完全相同的浪潮和市场顺风中。”。
进一步说，首次公开募股“肯定是摆在桌面上的，”汉迪说。这就是为什么他在新一轮中选择了多个交叉基金作为投资伙伴，因为它们的经验帮助公司实现了从私人到公共的市场转型。“说到底，这是一段非常漫长的旅程，我认为在未来三年内，标题（估值）数字将非常小，”他说。
note_en:
Dbt Labs cofounder and CEO Tristan Handy had lined up a slate of the top crossover funds to invest in his company at a $6.2 billion valuation when tech stocks took a sudden nosedive in January. As late-stage venture investors reassessed the lofty valuations they were handing out, Handy slashed his own target by nearly one third.
   The Philadelphia-based data analytics startup revealed Thursday that it had settled on a $4.2 billion valuation as part of a $222 million Series D funding round. Altimeter, which co-led the previous financing, is lead investor, with participation from existing backers Amplify Partners, Andreessen Horowitz and Sequoia. New investors include a trio of data cloud leaders in Databricks, Snowflake and Salesforce Ventures, plus Coatue, GIC, Iconiq and Tiger Global.
 Forbes first  reported in December that Dbt Labs was targeting a valuation of at least $6 billion. The lower valuation that it achieved in the end, Handy claims, had nothing to do with a shift in investor confidence. “They could have raised at any price they wanted,” affirms Altimeter partner Jamin Ball. “There was almost infinite demand.”
 “The investors were ready,” Handy says. “It’s that we have another set of stakeholders that we care about: the employees.” Employees can cash out on stock options if a startup goes public, but with tech stocks sinking, a valuation too high now could result in a lower payout upon IPO, he noted. “The last thing that we want is for our employees or the 300 people that we want to hire over the coming year to feel like we're not giving them the opportunity to fully participate in the upside,” he added. (Low code software startup  Retool recently used a similar argument.)
 This line of thinking may sound strange, Handy admits, especially in Silicon Valley—which, for the better part of the last year, has chased valuations to new heights. But based out of Philadelphia, where his wife is a doctor at Children’s Hospital, Handy has his own North Star. In 2016, he’d started the company as a small software consultancy after exhausting his hunt for an executive-level analytics job in the city’s nascent tech ecosystem. “‘Well, we’re not moving,’” Handy recalls his wife’s response. “So, I had to create that opportunity for myself.” Named Fishtown Analytics, a nod to a local blue-collar neighborhood, it set about supporting data processes for local companies.
  Along the way, Handy’s team built an internal “data build tool” (“dbt” for short) to improve the productivity of their consulting work. The tool piped in the many disparate types of raw data pouring in from across the cloud and democratized them so that they could be used for analytics. Consulting clients soon took notice of the internal tool, which Fishtown Analytics had made open source. By 2019, more than 1,000 businesses had adopted it and large enterprises began to call up Handy. “They were saying things like, ‘We want to use this thing, but you’re a 15-person company. I don’t know that we can trust such a critical piece of data infrastructure to such a tiny little company.’” The opportunity was obvious: Turn the free tool into software these companies would readily buy. Handy, who had bootstrapped his business until then, turned to the venture capitalists to fund the scale-up.
  Handy took his first check in April 2020 in an Andreessen Horowitz-led round that valued the company at $48 million, according to Pitchbook. It came right as enterprises, pushed by the pandemic, sped up their digital transformation efforts. With its data transformation tool, which multiple industry insiders have labeled as the market’s clear leader (unusual for a startup of its size), Handy’s startup established itself as a crucial component of the cloud data workflow. It became a unicorn—and rebranded to Dbt Labs—one year later. Even still, it’s growing faster than Handy anticipated. Last June, Handy announced plans to grow employee count to 250 by the end of 2022; his target has since doubled. About 9,000 companies use the open-source tool, more than 1,800 of which are paying customers in its hosted cloud service. Handy declined to comment on revenue, except to say it had grown sixfold in 2021, but two sources placed it in the double-digit millions.
 The latest capital injection will help Dbt Labs continue to scale up and meet growing demand, particularly abroad (less than 40% of users are based in North America). Now, Handy is looking towards building new software tools, namely one for standardizing business metrics. “The experience of a data professional today leaves a lot to be desired,” he says. “Instead of having a conversation about last month’s business performance, you end up talking about technical details, like how my revenue number differs from your revenue number. It’s a big waste of time and frequently it dissuades people from even trying to use data in the first place.”
 Such capabilities could be key to Handy’s ambitions of evolving Dbt Labs from a subscription product into “a foundational layer that things get built on,” much like new backers Snowflake and Databricks. Executives for both companies called their new investment a “vote of confidence” in Dbt Labs’ staying power in the ecosystem. And thanks to growing cloud demand, some  infrastructure providers are able to continue accelerating their growth, adds Altimeter’s Ball, even at the scale of billion-dollar yearly revenues. “Dbt in many ways is riding the exact same wave and market tailwind that someone like a Snowflake is [riding],” he says.
 Further down the line, an IPO is “certainly on the table,” Handy says. It’s why he picked multiple crossover funds as investment partners on the new round, given their experience helping companies make the private to public market transition. “At the end of the day, this is a very long journey that we are on, and I think the headline [valuation] number is going to feel very small in another three years,” he says.
title: 总部位于费城的Dbt实验室开发了一种开源数据分析工具，以Altimeter牵头的42亿美元估值，筹集了2.22亿美元的D系列
title_en: Philadelphia-based Dbt Labs, which develops an open-source data analytics tool, raises a $222M Series D at a $4.2B valuation led by Altimeter

Tumblr今天宣布，用户可以通过每月支付4.99美元或以39.99美元的折扣购买一年的无广告浏览来选择退出（桌面和移动应用程序上的）广告。
这意味着长期的海报可以回到2013年雅虎以11亿美元收购Tumblr、Tumblr禁止色情并失去大量观众、Verizon以原价的一小部分将Tumblr卖给WordPress所有者Automattic Inc.之前的样子。
创始人戴维·卡普（David Karp）反对允许广告，一旦该公司坚持这一原则，它就会被俗气、廉价的广告所困扰，这些广告似乎充斥着广告，而且往往毫无意义。但是现在，任何仍然使用Tumblr的人都可以重新获得该网站的无广告版本——如果他们付费的话。
与Tumblr处于巅峰时期相比，这一宣传的吸引力有所下降，但它确实为平台展示了新的生命迹象，这可能会被任何在过渡期徘徊的人所欣赏。当然，告诉长期用户Tumblr有未来的另一种方式是开发更多不花钱的新功能，而它在仪表板上的新博客查看器是一个不错的开始。对于下一步，这里有一个免费的想法：Tumblr NSFW，14.99美元，无广告，无障碍，价格低于高清Netflix。
note_en:
Tumblr  announced today that users can opt out of ads (on the desktop and in its mobile apps) by paying $4.99 per month or purchasing a year of ad-free browsing at a discount for $39.99.
   That means long-time posters can go back to the way things were  before Yahoo bought Tumblr in 2013 for $1.1 billion, before  Tumblr banned porn and lost a massive chunk of its audience, and before Verizon  sold Tumblr to WordPress owner Automattic Inc. for a tiny fraction of its original price.
 Founder David Karp was opposed to allowing advertising, and  once the company bent on that principle, it’s been plagued by tacky, cheap-looking advertisements that seemed to flood the feed and often make little to no sense. But now, anyone who still uses Tumblr can have the ad-free version of the site back — if they pay up.
 That pitch is a bit less appealing than it would’ve been when Tumblr was at its peak, but it does show new signs of life for the platform, that may be appreciated by anyone who’s stuck around through the transitions. Of course, another way to tell long-time users that there’s a future for Tumblr would be to develop more new features that don’t cost money, and its  new blog viewer on the dashboard is a decent start. For the next move, here’s a free idea: Tumblr NSFW, $14.99, ad-free and unblurred for less than the cost of  HD Netflix.
title: Tumblr在台式机和移动应用程序中推出了一款无广告层，每月4.99美元或每年39.99美元；Tumblr曾反对展示广告，但在2012年屈服了
title_en: Tumblr launches an ad-free tier for $4.99/month or $39.99/year on desktop and in its mobile apps; Tumblr was once opposed to displaying ads, but caved in 2012
